name: Aberfeldy
description: Aberfeldy is the spiritual home of Dewar's blended whisky and draws its water from the famous Pitilie Burn, known for deposits of alluvial gold. The distillery produces a honeyed, waxy Highland malt with notes of heather and orange peel, anchoring the Dewar's White Label and 12-year-old blends.

name: Aberlour
description: Aberlour distillery draws water from a natural spring in the heart of Speyside and is known for its rich, sherried style. The A'bunadh cask-strength range is particularly celebrated among whisky enthusiasts for its intense sherry character and non-chill-filtered presentation.

name: Amrut
description: Amrut in Bangalore was the first Indian distillery to release a single malt whisky for international markets in 2004. The high altitude and tropical heat of Bangalore produce an angel's share of 10-12% annually, resulting in intensely concentrated and richly flavored single malts.

name: Angel's Envy
description: Angel's Envy was founded by master distiller Lincoln Henderson, who had previously created Woodford Reserve. The brand pioneered the premium finished bourbon category, with its flagship bourbon finished in port wine barrels from Portugal. The distillery in downtown Louisville has become a major Bourbon Trail destination.

name: Ardbeg
description: Ardbeg produces some of the most heavily peated whiskies on Islay, yet achieves remarkable balance between smoke and sweetness. Rescued from closure in the 1990s, Ardbeg has built a cult following and its annual limited releases generate worldwide excitement.

name: Arran
description: Arran was the first legal distillery on the Isle of Arran in over 150 years when it opened in 1995 at Lochranza. Run independently, it has earned acclaim for its clean, fruity, and unpeated style. In 2019, a second distillery — Lagg — was opened on the island's south coast for peated production.

name: Auchentoshan
description: Auchentoshan is the only Scottish distillery to fully triple-distill every drop of spirit, following the traditional Lowland practice. Located on the outskirts of Glasgow, this produces an exceptionally smooth, light, and delicate malt that serves as an accessible entry point to single malt Scotch.

name: Balcones
description: Balcones was the first Texas whiskey distillery in modern times, founded in an old welding shop in Waco. Texas heat accelerates aging dramatically, and Balcones has won international acclaim for expressions made with blue corn, Hopi maize, and traditional single malt.

name: Ben Nevis
description: Ben Nevis distillery sits at the foot of Britain's highest mountain, Fort William, and is one of the oldest licensed distilleries in Scotland. Now owned by Japan's Nikka, it produces a robust, waxy Highland malt that is highly sought after by independent bottlers and Japanese whisky blenders alike.

name: Bladnoch
description: Bladnoch is Scotland's most southerly distillery, located in Wigtown, Galloway. After several closures and revivals, Australian entrepreneur David Prior acquired and restored the distillery in 2015. Bladnoch produces a light, floral Lowland style with citrus and vanilla notes.

name: Blair Athol
description: Blair Athol distillery in Pitlochry is one of the oldest working distilleries in Scotland, dating to 1798. Most of its production goes into the Bell's blended whisky, but its rare single malt releases — especially from the Flora & Fauna range — are prized for their nutty, sherried character.

name: Bowmore
description: Bowmore is the oldest licensed distillery on Islay and one of the oldest in Scotland. Situated on the shores of Loch Indaal, it produces a medium-peated style that bridges the gap between heavily peated south coast and lighter north coast Islay malts.

name: Breckenridge
description: Breckenridge Distillery operates at 9,600 feet elevation, making it one of the highest distilleries in the world. The high-altitude conditions and Rocky Mountain snowmelt water produce a distinctive bourbon with rich caramel and vanilla character. The distillery has expanded to a large facility and restaurant in Breckenridge.

name: Bruichladdich
description: Bruichladdich is the most westerly distillery in Scotland and produces three distinct styles: unpeated Bruichladdich, heavily peated Port Charlotte, and ultra-peated Octomore. The distillery is known for its focus on provenance, using 100% Scottish barley with terroir-driven releases.

name: Buffalo Trace
description: Buffalo Trace is one of America's oldest continuously operating distilleries, producing a vast portfolio that includes Pappy Van Winkle, Eagle Rare, Blanton's, and its eponymous bourbon. The distillery is a National Historic Landmark and has won more awards than any other North American distillery.

name: Bunnahabhain
description: Bunnahabhain is unique among Islay distilleries for producing predominantly unpeated single malt, with a nutty, sherried, and maritime character. The distillery sits at the head of a bay on Islay's northeast coast, drawing water from the Margadale spring.

name: Bushmills
description: Bushmills holds the oldest licensed distillery claim in the world, with a grant to distill dating to 1608 in County Antrim, Northern Ireland. The distillery triple-distills its single malts and uses a combination of bourbon and sherry casks for a smooth, honeyed character.

name: Canadian Club (Hiram Walker)
description: Canadian Club is produced at the historic Hiram Walker distillery in Windsor, Ontario, one of the largest distilleries in North America. Founded in 1858, the brand pioneered the practice of blending whisky before aging (pre-blending) and was a favorite spirit smuggled across the Detroit River during Prohibition.

name: Caol Ila
description: Caol Ila is the largest distillery on Islay by production capacity, yet much of its output traditionally goes into blends. Its single malt releases are prized for a lighter, more citrusy peat style with distinctive oily texture and a view across the Sound of Islay to Jura.

name: Cardhu
description: Cardhu holds a special place in whisky history as a distillery pioneered by Helen Cumming and her daughter-in-law Elizabeth, among the first women to run a Scottish distillery. It is the spiritual home of Johnnie Walker, providing the key malt component for the world's best-selling blended Scotch.

name: Castle & Key
description: Castle & Key occupies the beautifully restored Old Taylor distillery in Millville, Kentucky, a National Historic Landmark. The distillery focuses on small-batch bourbon and rye using heritage grain varieties and natural spring water. Its sunken gardens and castle-like architecture make it one of the most photogenic distilleries in America.

name: Chichibu
description: Chichibu is a small craft distillery founded by Ichiro Akuto in Saitama Prefecture. Despite its tiny output, Chichibu has become one of the most sought-after whiskies in the world, with releases selling at auction for thousands of dollars. The distillery uses Japanese mizunara casks and floor maltings.

name: Clynelish
description: Clynelish distillery in Brora produces a distinctively waxy, maritime single malt prized by independent bottlers and blenders alike. Built to replace the original Brora distillery, Clynelish's oily, coastal character with tropical fruit and beeswax notes has earned it a cult following.

name: Cragganmore
description: Cragganmore is regarded by many as the most complex of the Speyside malts, with a unique flat-topped still design that creates a distinctively herbaceous, floral, and multi-layered spirit. Selected by Diageo for the Classic Malts series as the Speyside representative, it remains a connoisseur's favorite.

name: Craigellachie
description: Craigellachie is one of only a handful of Scottish distilleries still using worm tubs instead of modern condensers, which gives its spirit a distinctive meaty, sulfury, and robust character. Long a blending workhorse, it has gained a cult single malt following for its unapologetically bold Speyside style.

name: Crown Royal
description: Crown Royal was created in 1939 to commemorate the visit of King George VI and Queen Elizabeth to Canada. Produced at the Gimli distillery in Manitoba on the shores of Lake Winnipeg, Crown Royal blends over 50 distinct whiskies to achieve its signature smooth, slightly sweet character in a distinctive purple bag.

name: Dalwhinnie
description: Dalwhinnie is the highest distillery in Scotland at 326 meters above sea level, situated at the gateway to the Highlands. Its extreme location creates a slow, gentle maturation that produces a delicate, honeyed malt. Selected as the Highland representative in Diageo's Classic Malts of Scotland.

name: Deanston
description: Deanston occupies a converted cotton mill on the River Teith in Perthshire, using its own hydroelectric turbine to power production — making it one of the most eco-friendly distilleries in Scotland. Its unchillfiltered, unpeated Highland malt offers a honeyed, malty character with a creamy texture.

name: Dingle
description: Dingle Distillery on the Wild Atlantic Way in County Kerry was one of the first wave of Ireland's craft distillery movement. Its small copper pot stills produce single malt and pot still whiskey in limited batches, with each release numbered and highly sought after by collectors.

name: Edradour
description: Edradour is one of the smallest traditional distilleries in Scotland, producing by hand what larger distilleries make in a week. Located in the picturesque village of Pitlochry, it was acquired by independent bottler Signatory Vintage and has gained acclaim for its sherried and wine-cask-finished releases.

name: Eigashima (White Oak)
description: Eigashima Shuzo in Akashi, Hyogo Prefecture holds Japan's first whisky license, granted in 1919 — four years before Yamazaki. The distillery primarily produces sake and shochu, with whisky made only a few months per year. Its Akashi single malt and blends are valued for their coastal, approachable character.

name: Four Roses
description: Four Roses is unique among Kentucky distillers in using 10 distinct bourbon recipes — two mashbills combined with five proprietary yeast strains. This approach allows exceptional blending complexity. The distillery's Spanish Mission-style architecture in Lawrenceburg is a Kentucky landmark.

name: George Dickel
description: George Dickel is Tennessee's other major whiskey producer, using a chilled charcoal mellowing process that founder George A. Dickel believed produced a smoother spirit. The distillery in Tullahoma spells its product 'whisky' without the 'e' as a nod to the Scottish tradition.

name: GlenAllachie
description: GlenAllachie was acquired in 2017 by former BenRiach master distiller Billy Walker, who transformed it from a blending workhorse into a celebrated single malt producer. The distillery holds one of the largest cask inventories in Speyside, with an emphasis on sherry-cask maturation and cask-strength releases.

name: GlenDronach
description: GlenDronach is celebrated for its dedication to sherry-cask maturation, using both Oloroso and Pedro Ximenez casks from Jerez. Restarted in 2002 after a silent period, the distillery has built a devoted following for its richly sherried, traditional Highland-Speyside character.

name: Glenfarclas
description: Glenfarclas has been owned by six generations of the Grant family, making it one of the few truly independent Speyside distilleries. Known for its direct-fired stills and exclusive use of sherry casks, Glenfarclas produces rich, full-bodied malts with its legendary Family Casks series spanning vintages from the 1950s onward.

name: Glenfiddich
description: Glenfiddich is the world's best-selling single malt Scotch whisky, pioneering the category's global growth since the 1960s. The family-owned distillery in Dufftown remains one of the few Scottish distilleries with its own on-site cooperage and coppersmith.

name: Glengoyne
description: Glengoyne sits on the Highland Line boundary, distilling in the Highlands but maturing its whisky across the road in the Lowlands. It is notable for using completely unpeated barley dried with warm air only, and exclusively sherry casks for maturation, producing an exceptionally clean, rich, and fruity malt.

name: Glen Grant
description: Glen Grant is one of the best-selling single malts in the world, particularly dominant in the Italian market. Founded by brothers John and James Grant in Rothes, the distillery is known for its tall, slender stills with purifiers that produce a light, crisp, and apple-forward spirit.

name: Glenkinchie
description: Glenkinchie is known as 'The Edinburgh Malt,' situated just 15 miles from the Scottish capital in the rolling farmland of East Lothian. Selected as the Lowland representative in Diageo's Classic Malts, it produces a light, grassy, and floral spirit with one of the largest wash stills in Scotland.

name: Glenmorangie
description: Glenmorangie operates the tallest pot stills in Scotland, standing over 5 meters high, which produce an exceptionally light and elegant spirit. The distillery pioneered wood-finishing in the whisky industry and its core range includes finishes in Sauternes, sherry, and Burgundy casks.

name: Glen Scotia
description: Glen Scotia is one of only three remaining distilleries in Campbeltown, once Scotland's whisky capital with over 30 distilleries. Revived under the Loch Lomond Group, Glen Scotia produces a maritime, briny style with gentle peat smoke that captures the essence of the Campbeltown region.

name: Hakushu
description: Hakushu distillery sits in a forest at 700 meters elevation in the Japanese Southern Alps, producing a distinctively fresh, herbal, and lightly smoky style. The high-altitude forest setting provides crisp mountain water and cool temperatures ideal for delicate spirit production.

name: Heaven Hill
description: Heaven Hill is the largest independent, family-owned distillery in the United States, producing brands including Elijah Craig, Evan Williams, Larceny, and Henry McKenna. The Shapira family has owned the company since its founding, making it one of Kentucky's most significant bourbon producers.

name: Hellyers Road
description: Hellyers Road in Burnie is Tasmania's largest whisky distillery, owned by a local dairy co-operative. The distillery produces both peated and unpeated single malts using Tasmanian barley and pure water from the region's ancient rainforests. Its output has grown from craft-scale to one of Australia's most widely available single malts.

name: Highland Park
description: Highland Park is the northernmost Scotch whisky distillery, located in Kirkwall, Orkney. The distillery hand-turns its own locally cut Orkney peat on traditional floor maltings, creating a heather-honey sweetness layered with gentle smoke that defines the Highland Park style.

name: Hudson
description: Hudson Whiskey from Tuthilltown Spirits in the Hudson Valley was one of the first craft whiskeys to challenge the Kentucky establishment. Its Baby Bourbon — aged in small barrels for accelerated maturation — helped launch the American craft distilling revolution. The distillery now produces a full range of bourbon, rye, and single malt.

name: Jack Daniel's
description: Jack Daniel's is the world's best-selling American whiskey, produced in Lynchburg, Tennessee since 1866. Every drop is charcoal-mellowed through 10 feet of sugar maple charcoal before aging, a process that gives Tennessee whiskey its distinctive smooth character.

name: Jim Beam
description: Jim Beam is the world's best-selling bourbon brand, produced by the Beam family for seven generations in Clermont, Kentucky. The distillery uses a proprietary yeast strain that has been maintained since before Prohibition, contributing to its signature smooth, caramel-forward character.

name: Jura
description: The Isle of Jura distillery is the only distillery on the remote island, which has fewer than 200 residents but over 6,000 red deer. Rebuilt in 1963 to bring employment to the island, Jura produces both unpeated and lightly peated expressions with a distinctive oily, coastal character.
